{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Alekhine's Defence| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd| |rd|= |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | |nd| | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | |pl| | | |= | | |nl| | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} =Alekhine's Defence= White has refused to take the risk to push his pawn forward. Instead, he chose to defend it. This choice can be seen as "evasive". Black might think that White is trying to avoid [[Chess/Alekhine's Defence|Alekhine's Defence]]. They may accept to transpose into [[Chess/Vienna Game|Vienna Game]] with [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...e5/2. Nc3/2...Nf6|2...e5]] or they may continue in a sharper line with [[/2...d5/]]. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}}. '1.e4 Nf6 2.Nc3'
